You need to improve E2E test coverage to ≥95% on all metrics (statements, branches, functions, lines) in a NestJS + Fastify backend.

## Working Directory
`/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/no-name-proj/backend`

## Current E2E coverage gaps (per file)

These files need more coverage:

| File | Stmts | Branch | Funcs | Uncovered lines |
|------|-------|--------|-------|-----------------|
| session.service.ts | 75% | 56% | 76% | 64-68,72,91,120,135-138,142,163-187,207-208,263-264,291 |
| company-member.service.ts | 64% | 100% | 44% | 35-43,55-75 |
| oauth-account.service.ts | 72% | 100% | 50% | 26-34,48 |
| user.service.ts | 81% | 100% | 66% | 24-28 |
| auth.service.ts | 93% | 79% | 100% | 79,131-146,300,350 |
| auth.controller.ts | 98% | 87% | 100% | 102 |
| company.service.ts | 88% | 100% | 75% | 24 |
| company.controller.ts | 94% | 75% | 100% | 43 |
| invitation.controller.ts | 96% | 75% | 100% | 99 |

## Your job

1. Read each low-coverage file to understand what the uncovered lines do
2. Read the corresponding E2E spec files to understand what's already tested
3. Add test cases to the existing E2E spec files to cover the gaps
4. Run `npm run test:e2e:coverage` and verify all metrics hit ≥95%
5. Fix any test failures you encounter
6. Commit

## E2E spec files to modify
- `test/auth.module.e2e-spec.ts`
- `test/user.module.e2e-spec.ts`
- `test/company.module.e2e-spec.ts`
- `test/invitation.module.e2e-spec.ts`

## How to run coverage
```bash
npm run test:e2e:coverage 2>&1 | grep -E "^\s+(All files|\w)" | head -40
```

## How to run just one spec file during debugging
```bash
dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --runInBand --forceExit --config ./test/jest-e2e.config.ts test/auth.module.e2e-spec.ts
```

## Code style rules (STRICTLY ENFORCED)
- NO comments in code
- NO relative imports — use `@aliases` only
- All custom functions use single object destructuring parameter
- Explicit return types on all functions
- `tsconfig.test.json` relaxes strict checks for test files

## Important: E2E test patterns

The tests use real PostgreSQL + Redis (`.env.test`). The app is bootstrapped with `AppModule` and Fastify adapter.

To create authenticated requests, use the `createAccessToken` helper from `test/helpers/auth.helper.ts`.

After adding tests, run the full coverage check:
```bash
npm run test:e2e:coverage 2>&1 | tail -10
```

Look for the threshold failure output — it will show exactly which metrics are still below 95%.

## Commit when done
```bash
git add test/
git commit -m "test: add E2E tests to meet 95% coverage threshold

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

## Status
End with:
- `DONE` — all E2E coverage ≥95%, committed
- `DONE_WITH_CONCERNS: <details>`
- `BLOCKED: <reason>`
